title: GitHub - aido-dev/aido: Meet Aido — your open-source, multi-provider AI coding assistant for GitHub. Run code reviews, summaries, explanations, and code improvements directly from pull request comments using aido <command>. Fully modular, configurable, and extensible to fit your workflows.

content_html:
The AI companion for the pull requests your AI writes.

AI agents — Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor — are opening more and more pull requests, and a human still has to understand code they didn't write. Aido keeps that human in the loop: when an AI-authored PR lands, it can automatically explain, summarize, review, and document the change. And you can run those same commands on any PR or issue on demand — just comment aido <command>.

One companion, the whole review lifecycle: review, summarize, explain, document, test, and triage — with Gemini, ChatGPT, or Claude, right inside GitHub Actions. Install with a single workflow file.

✨ Highlights

  • 🤖 Auto-companion for AI-authored PRs — when Copilot / Claude Code / Cursor open a PR, Aido runs automatically (explain + summarize by default; review/docs/test opt-in)
  • ⚡ On-demand on any PR or issue — aido review, summarize, explain, docs, suggest, test, triage
  • 🧩 Consolidated, persona-guided reviewer with applyable inline suggestions (robust validation, zero false positives)
  • 🔌 Multi-provider, bring-your-own-key: Gemini (default), ChatGPT, Claude — no third-party data processor
  • 📦 One-file install from a pinned release tag; upgrading is a one-line bump
  • 🔧 Fully configurable prompts, personas, tones, and per-command models

Requirements

  • Secrets (add under Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions):
    • GEMINI_API_KEY (required for default provider)
    • CHATGPT_API_KEY (if using ChatGPT)
    • CLAUDE_API_KEY (if using Claude)
  • Uses the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN for posting comments and reviews.
  • ⚠️ Forked PRs: repository secrets may be unavailable due to GitHub policy.

📝 Example Commands

Comment these on any PR:

  • aido review → Multi-persona code review + digest
  • aido summarize | aido sum | aido summary → High-level PR summary for stakeholders
  • aido explain → Developer-focused step-by-step explanation
  • aido docs → Draft/augment documentation
  • aido suggest | aido improve → Safe improvement ideas
  • aido test → Structured test plan, coverage gaps, and follow-up tasks
  • aido config-check → Validate configs

Comment these on any issue:

  • aido triage → Classify, suggest labels, find similar issues, recommend next steps

🤖 Auto-run on AI-authored PRs

When an AI agent (Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, …) opens a pull request, Aido can run automatically — no comment needed — so a human can quickly understand and digest code they didn't write.

  • Add .github/workflows/aido-auto.yml (copy-based) or examples/remote/aido-auto.yml (remote install).
  • Configure which authors trigger it and which commands run in .github/scripts/auto/aido-auto-config.json.
  • Companion-first defaults: explain + summarize. Add review, docs, or test to the commands list to run more.
  • Only AI-authored PRs trigger it (per aiAuthors); human PRs are never auto-run. It fires on PR open/reopen/ready — not on every commit.
  • Per-PR opt-out: add a no-aido label (configurable via skipLabels) or put <!-- aido: skip --> in the PR body to skip a single PR — no config change needed.
  • Fires on pull_request (not pull_request_target), so forked PRs stay safe (read-only token, no secrets).

Note: github-actions[bot] and dependabot[bot] are excluded by default — the former is too broad, and Dependabot PRs run with a read-only token and no repo secrets, so Aido can't act on them. Add them explicitly at your own risk.

🧩 Use Aido as a GitHub Action (a step in your workflow)

Prefer to control exactly when Aido runs? Add it as a step in your own workflow — the Marketplace-published composite action:

- uses: aido-dev/aido@v1
  with:
    command: review # review | summarize | explain | docs | suggest | test | triage
    pr_number: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  env:
    GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    GEMINI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GEMINI_API_KEY }}

For triage, pass issue_number instead of pr_number. See examples/action/ for full workflows.

Two ways to run Aido — pick per use case:

  • Reusable workflows / one-file install (below) → the comment-driven UX (aido review on a PR) and auto-run on AI-authored PRs.
  • This composite action → run a specific command as a step, on your own triggers (e.g. review every PR on pull_request).

🚀 Quick Start

Option A — Remote install (one file, recommended)

  1. Add repository secrets:
    • GEMINI_API_KEY (default provider)
    • CHATGPT_API_KEY (if using ChatGPT)
    • CLAUDE_API_KEY (if using Claude)
  2. Copy examples/remote/aido.yml to .github/workflows/aido.yml — a single thin workflow that runs Aido from a pinned release tag. Upgrading is a one-line tag bump.
  3. Comment aido review on a PR.
  4. (Optional) Customize any command by adding its config file (e.g. .github/scripts/review/aido-review-config.json) — overrides the shipped defaults, no scripts needed. See examples/remote/ for details.

Option B — Copy-based install (full control)

  1. Add repository secrets (as above).
  2. Commit workflows (.github/workflows/*) and scripts (.github/scripts/*). ⚠️ Make sure to include .github/scripts/lib/ — all command scripts depend on this shared library.
  3. Comment aido review on a PR.
  4. (Optional) Customize configs in aido-*-config.json — or the prompts and scripts themselves.

📚 Learn More

  • Workflows
  • Scripts & Configs
  • Persona Packs

🆚 Summarize vs Explain

  • Audience:
    • Summarize: stakeholders (product/engineering leadership)
    • Explain: developers/reviewers
  • Depth:
    • Summarize: high-level intent, scope, risks, impact; no implementation details
    • Explain: step-by-step mechanics, rationale, risks, verification
  • Content constraints:
    • Summarize: no code blocks, diffs, or inline suggestions; concise and skimmable
    • Explain: may include tiny illustrative snippets only if essential; avoid suggestions and large blocks

❤️ Why Aido?

As more of your PRs are written by AI, Aido makes sure a human still understands them — automatically, and on demand. It's practical, configurable, and team-friendly: save time, catch issues early, and keep everyone in the loop — without leaving GitHub.

How It Works

  • Dispatcher: .github/workflows/aido-dispatch.yml Parses the first line of the comment, normalizes it, and routes to the right reusable workflow.
  • Reusable workflows (invoked via workflow_call):
    • .github/workflows/aido-review.yml
    • .github/workflows/aido-summarize.yml
    • .github/workflows/aido-explain.yml
    • .github/workflows/aido-docs.yml
    • .github/workflows/aido-suggest.yml
    • .github/workflows/aido-test.yml
    • .github/workflows/aido-triage.yml (issues)

Each workflow builds a prompt from PR context (title, body, changed files, truncated diff ~15k chars), calls the selected provider/model, and posts a PR review with inline, applyable suggestions (when applicable), validated by a robust layer to ensure safety and accuracy.

Scripts & Configs

  • Shared library (required by all commands): .github/scripts/lib/
    • providers.js — AI provider wrappers (ChatGPT / Gemini / Claude), model resolution
    • github.js — GitHub API client, event parsing, PR context fetchers, comment posting
    • config.js — JSON config loading with defaults and deep merge
    • text.js — truncation, files summary, prompt templates, comment footers
  • Review:
    • Script: .github/scripts/review/aido-review.js
    • Config: .github/scripts/review/aido-review-config.json (object with { reviewer, personas }; reviewer sets provider/model; personas guide facets)
  • Summarize (stakeholder-facing; aliases: summarize | sum | summary):
    • Script: .github/scripts/summarize/aido-summarize.js
    • Config: .github/scripts/summarize/aido-summarize-config.json
  • Explain (developer-focused):
    • Script: .github/scripts/explain/aido-explain.js
    • Config: .github/scripts/explain/aido-explain-config.json
  • Docs:
    • Script: .github/scripts/docs/aido-docs.js
    • Config: .github/scripts/docs/aido-docs-config.json
  • Suggest:
    • Script: .github/scripts/suggest/aido-suggest.js
    • Config: .github/scripts/suggest/aido-suggest-config.json
  • Test:
    • Script: .github/scripts/test/aido-test.js
    • Config: .github/scripts/test/aido-test-config.json (adds testFocus for unit / integration / e2e / regression / performance / security / accessibility)
  • Triage (issues):
    • Script: .github/scripts/triage/aido-triage.js
    • Config: .github/scripts/triage/aido-triage-config.json (adds candidateLabels, severityLabels, and applyLabels to optionally auto-apply suggested labels; default false)

Each config supports: provider (CHATGPT|GEMINI|CLAUDE), model, language, tone, style, length, include (title/body/filesSummary/diff), additionalInstructions, and an optional promptTemplate with placeholders.

Persona Reviews (Aido Review)

  • Use a single consolidated reviewer informed by your configured personas in aido-review-config.json.
    • Top-level reviewer chooses provider/model (and optional context checks).
    • personas define roles with prompt/tone/style/language to guide faceted notes.
  • The review body contains a clean summary, recommendation, faceted notes, and optional context checks.
  • All code changes are delivered as inline PR review suggestions (with “Apply suggestion” buttons), thoroughly validated for safety and actionability — not in the body.
  • Keep it reasonable: start with 3–5 personas (e.g., pedagogy, architecture, security, performance, QA).

Pre-curated packs: see /examples/.github/review/example personas/

  • example-personas.json (~50 personas)
  • great_defaults-personas.json (balanced starter set)
  • Topic packs: web_frontend-*.json, cloud_and_devops-*.json, security-*.json, and more.

Tips

  • Prefer short, focused prompts and configs.
  • Use aido config-check if things look off.
  • For UI work, pair aido explain with aido suggest.
  • For releases, run aido summarize → aido docs.
  • Before merging, run aido test to surface missing test cases and coverage gaps.
  • For new issues, run aido triage to get a quick classification, label suggestions, and similar-issue links.

Caveats

  • Diff is truncated (~15k chars) to keep prompts efficient.
  • Provider/model availability and naming can change; set explicit models in configs.
  • Forked PRs may lack secrets → provider calls may be skipped.

🔖 Topics

github-actions · ai-code-review · ai-assistant · developer-productivity · persona-based-reviews · openai · gemini · claude · chatgpt · pr-bot · automated-code-review

Happy shipping! ✨

Note

Data handling:

  • Prompts, code, and metadata may be sent to external AI services and could be logged or retained by those providers.
  • Do not include secrets, confidential, or regulated data unless you fully trust the operator and provider.

Reliability:

  • Providers can rate‑limit, change models/behavior, or go offline without notice.
  • Do not depend on AI outputs for production without human review and validation.

Accuracy:

  • AI models can be incorrect, outdated, or hallucinate details.
  • Always verify explanations, reviews, and code suggestions before applying.

blocks.html_en: When an AI agent (Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, …) opens a pull request, Aido can run automatically — no comment needed — so a human can quickly understand and digest code they didn't write.

sentences.en: When an AI agent (Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, …)

sentences.en: opens a pull request, Aido can run automatically — no comment needed — so a human can quickly understand and digest code they didn't write.

sentences.zh: 将评论、文档或测试添加到命令列表以运行更多命令。

blocks.html_en: • Only AI-authored PRs trigger it (per aiAuthors); human PRs are never auto-run. It fires on PR open/reopen/ready — not on every commit.

sentences.zh: 只有 AI 创作的 PR 才会触发它（根据 aiAuthors）；人类 PR 永远不会自动运行。

sentences.zh: 它在 PR 打开/重新打开/准备就绪时触发，而不是在每次提交时触发。

blocks.html_en: • Per-PR opt-out: add a no-aido label (configurable via skipLabels) or put <!-- aido: skip --> in the PR body to skip a single PR — no config change needed.

sentences.zh: 在 pull_request （而不是 pull_request_target）上触发，因此分叉的 PR 保持安全（只读令牌，没有秘密）。

Two ways to run Aido — pick per use case:

  • This composite action → run a specific command as a step, on your own triggers (e.g. review every PR on pull_request).

blocks.html_en: ❤️ Why Aido?

sentences.en: ❤️ Why Aido?
sentences.zh: ❤️ 为什么是爱多？

blocks.html_en: As more of your PRs are written by AI, Aido makes sure a human still understands them — automatically, and on demand. It's practical, configurable, and team-friendly: save time, catch issues early, and keep everyone in the loop — without leaving GitHub.

sentences.en: As more of your PRs are written by AI, Aido makes sure a human still understands them — automatically, and on demand.
sentences.zh: 随着越来越多的 PR 是由 AI 编写的，Aido 确保人们仍然可以自动且按需理解它们。

sentences.en: It's practical, configurable, and team-friendly: save time, catch issues early, and keep everyone in the loop — without leaving GitHub.
sentences.zh: 它实用、可配置且适合团队使用：节省时间、尽早发现问题并让每个人都了解情况 - 无需离开 GitHub。

blocks.html_en: Each workflow builds a prompt from PR context (title, body, changed files, truncated diff ~15k chars), calls the selected provider/model, and posts a PR review with inline, applyable suggestions (when applicable), validated by a robust layer to ensure safety and accuracy.

blocks.html_en: Scripts & Configs

sentences.en: Scripts & Configs
sentences.zh: 脚本和配置

blocks.html_en:
• Shared library (required by all commands): .github/scripts/lib/
    • providers.js — AI provider wrappers (ChatGPT / Gemini / Claude), model resolution
    • github.js — GitHub API client, event parsing, PR context fetchers, comment posting
    • config.js — JSON config loading with defaults and deep merge

content_html_zh:
AI 编写的拉取请求的 AI 伴侣。

人工智能代理——Copilot、Claude Code、Cursor——正在打开越来越多的拉取请求，而人类仍然必须理解他们没有编写的代码。 Aido 让人类随时了解情况：当人工智能撰写的 PR 落地时，它可以自动解释、总结、审查和记录更改。 您可以根据需要在任何 PR 或问题上运行相同的命令 - 只需注释 aido <command>。

一个伴侣，整个审阅生命周期：审阅、总结、解释、记录、测试和分类 — 使用 Gemini、ChatGPT 或 Claude，就在 GitHub Actions 内。 使用单个工作流程文件进行安装。

✨ 亮点

  • 🤖 AI 创作的 PR 的自动陪伴 — 当 Copilot / Claude Code / Cursor 打开 PR 时，Aido 会自动运行（默认解释 + 总结；审查/文档/测试选择加入）
  • ⚡ 按需处理任何 PR 或问题 — aido 审查、总结、解释、文档、建议、测试、分类
  • 🧩 综合的、以角色为导向的审阅者，具有适用的内联建议（强大的验证，零误报）
  • 🔌 多提供商，自带密钥：Gemini（默认）、ChatGPT、Claude — 无第三方数据处理器
  • 📦 从固定发布标签进行单文件安装；升级是一条直线
  • 🔧 完全可配置的提示、角色、音调和每个命令模型

要求

  • 秘密（在“设置”→“秘密和变量”→“操作”下添加）： GEMINI_API_KEY（默认提供商所需） CHATGPT_API_KEY（如果使用 ChatGPT） CLAUDE_API_KEY（如果使用 Claude）
  • 使用内置的 GITHUB_TOKEN 来发表评论和评论。
  • ⚠️ 分叉 PR：由于 GitHub 政策，存储库机密可能不可用。

📝 示例命令

在任何 PR 上评论这些：

  • aido 审查 → 多角色代码审查+摘要
  • aido总结|爱多森 | aido 摘要 → 利益相关者的高级公关摘要
  • aido 解释 → 以开发人员为中心的分步解释
  • aido 文档 → 草稿/扩充文档
  • aido建议| aido Improve → 安全改进思路
  • aido 测试 → 结构化测试计划、覆盖范围差距和后续任务
  • aido 配置检查 → 验证配置

对任何问题发表评论：

  • aido 分类 → 分类、建议标签、查找类似问题、推荐后续步骤

🤖 在 AI 创作的 PR 上自动运行

当人工智能代理（副驾驶、克劳德代码、光标……） 打开拉取请求，Aido 可以自动运行 - 无需注释 - 因此人们可以快速理解和消化他们没有编写的代码。

  • 添加 .github/workflows/aido-auto.yml （基于副本）或 Examples/remote/aido-auto.yml （远程安装）。
  • 配置哪些作者触发它以及哪些命令在 .github/scripts/auto/aido-auto-config.json 中运行。
  • 同伴优先默认值：解释+总结。 将评论、文档或测试添加到命令列表以运行更多命令。
  • 只有 AI 创作的 PR 才会触发它（根据 aiAuthors）；人类 PR 永远不会自动运行。 它在 PR 打开/重新打开/准备就绪时触发，而不是在每次提交时触发。
  • 按 PR 选择退出：添加 no-aido 标签（可通过skipLabels 配置）或将 <!-- aido:skip --> 放入 PR 正文中以跳过单个 PR — 无需更改配置。
  • 在 pull_request （而不是 pull_request_target）上触发，因此分叉的 PR 保持安全（只读令牌，没有秘密）。
注意：默认情况下会排除 github-actions[bot] 和 dependentabot[bot] - 前者太宽泛，并且 Dependabot PR 使用只读令牌运行，没有存储库机密，因此 Aido 无法对它们采取行动。 明确添加它们的风险由您自行承担。

🧩 使用 Aido 作为 GitHub Action（工作流程中的一个步骤）

想要精确控制 Aido 何时运行？ 将其添加为您自己的工作流程中的一个步骤 - Marketplace 发布的复合操作：

- uses: aido-dev/aido@v1
  with:
    command: review # review | summarize | explain | docs | suggest | test | triage
    pr_number: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  env:
    GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    GEMINI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GEMINI_API_KEY }}

对于分类，请传递 issues_number 而不是 pr_number。 请参阅示例/操作/了解完整的工作流程。

运行 Aido 的两种方法 - 根据用例选择：可重用工作流程/单文件安装（如下）→ 评论驱动的 UX（aido 对 PR 进行审查）和在 AI 创作的 PR 上自动运行。 此复合操作 → 在您自己的触发器上运行特定命令作为步骤（例如，查看 pull_request 上的每个 PR）。

🚀 快速入门

选项 A — 远程安装（推荐一个文件）

  1. 添加存储库机密：GEMINI_API_KEY（默认提供程序） CHATGPT_API_KEY（如果使用 ChatGPT） CLAUDE_API_KEY（如果使用 Claude）
  2. 将 example/remote/aido.yml 复制到 .github/workflows/aido.yml — 一个从固定发布标签运行 Aido 的精简工作流程。 升级是单行标记升级。
  3. 对 PR 发表评论 aido 评论。
  4. （可选）通过添加配置文件（例如 .github/scripts/review/aido-review-config.json）来自定义任何命令 - 覆盖附带的默认值，无需脚本。 有关详细信息，请参阅示例/远程/。

选项 B — 基于副本的安装（完全控制）

  1. 添加存储库机密（如上所述）。
  2. 提交工作流程 (.github/workflows/*) 和脚本 (.github/scripts/*)。 ⚠️ 确保包含 .github/scripts/lib/ — 所有命令脚本都依赖于这个共享库。
  3. 对 PR 发表评论 aido 评论。
  4. （可选）在 aido-*-config.json 中自定义配置 - 或提示和脚本本身。

🆚 总结与解释

  • 听众：总结：利益相关者（产品/工程领导）解释：开发人员/审阅者
  • 深度：总结：高层意图、范围、风险、影响；无实施细节 解释：分步机制、基本原理、风险、验证
  • 内容限制：总结：没有代码块、差异或内联建议；简洁易读的解释：仅在必要时才可以包含微小的说明性片段；避免建议和大块

❤️ 为什么是爱多？

随着越来越多的 PR 是由 AI 编写的，Aido 确保人们仍然可以自动且按需理解它们。 它实用、可配置且适合团队使用：节省时间、尽早发现问题并让每个人都了解情况 - 无需离开 GitHub。

它是如何运作的

  • 调度程序：.github/workflows/aido-dispatch.yml 解析注释的第一行，对其进行规范化，然后路由到正确的可重用工作流程。
  • 可重用工作流程（通过workflow_call调用）： .github/workflows/aido-review.yml .github/workflows/aido-summarize.yml .github/workflows/aido-explain.yml .github/workflows/aido-docs.yml .github/workflows/aido-suggest.yml .github/workflows/aido-test.yml .github/workflows/aido-triage.yml（问题）

每个工作流程都会根据 PR 上下文（标题、正文、更改的文件、截断的差异 ~15k 字符）构建提示，调用选定的提供商/模型，并发布带有内联、适用建议（如果适用）的 PR 审查，并由强大的层进行验证，以确保安全性和准确性。

脚本和配置

  • 共享库（所有命令都需要）： .github/scripts/lib/providers.js — AI 提供程序包装器（ChatGPT / Gemini / Claude），模型解析 github.js — GitHub API 客户端、事件解析、PR 上下文获取器、评论发布 config.js — 使用默认值和深度合并加载 JSON 配置 text.js — 截断、文件摘要、提示模板、评论页脚
  • 评论：脚本：.github/scripts/review/aido-review.js 配置：.github/scripts/review/aido-review-config.json（带有 { reviewer, personas } 的对象；reviewer 设置提供者/模型；角色指南方面）
  • 总结（面向利益相关者；别名：summary | sum | summarize）：脚本：.github/scripts/summarize/aido-summarize.js 配置：.github/scripts/summarize/aido-summarize-config.json
  • 解释（以开发人员为中心）：脚本：.github/scripts/explain/aido-explain.js 配置：.github/scripts/explain/aido-explain-config.json
  • 文档：脚本：.github/scripts/docs/aido-docs.js 配置：.github/scripts/docs/aido-docs-config.json
  • 建议：脚本：.github/scripts/suggest/aido-suggest.js 配置：.github/scripts/suggest/aido-suggest-config.json
  • 测试：脚本：.github/scripts/test/aido-test.js 配置：.github/scripts/test/aido-test-config.json（为单元/集成/e2e/回归/性能/安全/可访问性添加 testFocus）
  • 分类（问题）：脚本：.github/scripts/triage/aido-triage.js 配置：.github/scripts/triage/aido-triage-config.json（添加 CandidateLabels、severityLabels 和 applyLabels 来选择自动应用建议标签；默认 false）
每个配置支持：提供商 (CHATGPT|GEMINI|CLAUDE)、模型、语言、语气、风格、长度、包含 (标题/正文/文件摘要/差异)、附加说明和带有占位符的可选提示模板。

角色评论（Aido 评论）

  • 使用由 aido-review-config.json 中配置的角色通知的单个综合审阅者。 顶级审核者选择提供者/模型（以及可选的上下文检查）。 人物角色用提示/语气/风格/语言来定义角色，以指导多面注释。
  • 审查正文包含简洁的摘要、建议、多面注释和可选的上下文检查。
  • 所有代码更改均作为内联 PR 审查建议（带有“应用建议”按钮）提供，并经过彻底验证以确保安全性和可操作性，而不是在正文中。
  • 保持合理：从 3-5 个角色开始（例如教学法、架构、安全性、性能、QA）。

预先策划的包：请参阅 /examples/.github/review/example personas/

  • example-people.json（约 50 人）
  • Great_defaults-personas.json（平衡的入门集）
  • 主题包：web_frontend-*.json、cloud_and_devops-*.json、security-*.json 等。

Tips

  • 喜欢简短、集中的提示和配置。
  • 使用 aido config-check 是否出现问题。
  • 对于 UI 工作，将 aidoterpret 和 aido suggest 配对。
  • 对于版本，运行 aido summarise → aido docs。
  • 在合并之前，运行 aido 测试以找出缺失的测试用例和覆盖范围差距。
  • 对于新问题，运行 aido triage 以获得快速分类、标签建议和类似问题链接。

注意事项

  • Diff 被截断（约 15k 个字符）以保持提示高效。
  • 提供商/型号的可用性和命名可能会发生变化；在配置中设置显式模型。
  • 分叉的 PR 可能缺乏秘密 → 提供商调用可能会被跳过。

Note

数据处理：

  • 提示、代码和元数据可以发送到外部人工智能服务，并可以由这些提供商记录或保留。
  • 除非您完全信任运营商和提供商，否则请勿包含秘密、机密或受监管数据。

可靠性：

  • 提供商可以限制速率、更改模型/行为或下线，恕不另行通知。
  • 不要在没有人工审查和验证的情况下依赖人工智能输出进行生产。

准确性：

  • 人工智能模型可能不正确、过时或产生幻觉的细节。
  • 在申请之前务必验证解释、评论和代码建议。
